title: Croatian to B1 — the validated path

days.title: Welcome to B1 — aspect in context
days.objective: Reactivate perfective/imperfective pairs and start your daily native-input habit.
days.paretoFocus: B1 is won by verbs-in-context and native input. From today: one HRT news item every single day, no exceptions.
drills: Listen to 1 short HRT news item; note the 3 main facts.
Review 10 aspect pairs you know (pisati/napisati, čitati/pročitati…).
For each pair, say one sentence in each aspect.
Skim the B1 topics in the Grammar tab so you know the road ahead.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Explain aloud: when do you use the perfective aspect?
Retell today's news item in 2 sentences.

days.title: Verb family: pisati
days.objective: Master the pisati family: napisati, opisati, zapisati, potpisati, prepisati.
days.paretoFocus: One root + a handful of prefixes = five verbs. Croatian builds its verb lexicon this way — learn families, not lone verbs.
drills: Open the Words tab: verb-families pack — study the pisati family.
Learn the meaning shift of each prefix: na- (complete), o- (describe), za- (note down), pot- (sign), pre- (copy).
Write one sentence per family member.
Listen to 1 HRT news item; spot any pisati-family verb.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
From memory: what does opisati vs zapisati mean?
Say 'I signed the contract' (Potpisao/Potpisala sam ugovor).

days.title: Choosing the aspect in real sentences
days.objective: Pick pisati vs napisati (and friends) correctly in context.
days.paretoFocus: The exam tests exactly this: the right prefixed/aspect verb dropped into a sentence. Train the choice, not just the list.
drills: Take 8 sentences and choose the correct pisati-family verb for each.
Open the Grammar tab → A2 · verb aspect; copy out 3 pairs.
Listen to 1 HRT news item; write down every verb you catch.
Turn 3 imperfective sentences into perfective ones.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Explain: 'Pisao sam pismo' vs 'Napisao sam pismo'.
Review the pisati family from memory.

days.title: Suvremeni svijet — modern life vocabulary
days.objective: Talk about modern life, standards of living and screen addiction.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO's biggest B1 theme block. This vocabulary powers opinions — the core B1 speaking skill.
drills: Learn 15 theme words: suvremen, ovisnost, mobitel, društvene mreže, standard, navika…
Speaking session #1 this week: discuss screen time with your tutor or your Croatian spouse.
Express one opinion with 'Mislim da…' and defend it for 1 minute.
Listen to 1 HRT news item.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give your opinion on social media in 3 sentences.
Recall 10 of today's theme words.

days.title: Reading a column
days.objective: Read a short opinion column and guess unknown words from context.
days.paretoFocus: Columns and interviews are the exam's reading genres. Guessing from context is the single highest-value reading strategy.
drills: Read one short column on a news portal about modern life.
Underline 5 unknown words; guess each from context BEFORE looking them up.
Summarize the author's stance in 2 Croatian sentences.
Ask your partner one vocabulary question if something stays unclear.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
How many of your 5 guesses were right?
Retell the column's main point aloud.

days.title: Writing: a personal letter
days.objective: Write a personal letter (10+ sentences) about your life lately.
days.paretoFocus: Personal letters are an exam writing genre. Weekly writing turns passive grammar into active skill.
drills: Write a letter to a friend: news, plans, one retold event — 10+ sentences.
Use at least 3 pisati-family verbs and 2 aspect pairs.
Check openings/closings: Draga/Dragi…, Srdačan pozdrav.
Ask your partner to correct it.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-read your letter aloud, fixing anything that sounds off.
Note 3 corrections to re-test tomorrow.

days.title: Week 22 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate the week and hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Weekly consolidation plus real conversation locks the week's grammar into speech.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min) with your tutor or Croatian spouse: modern life and habits.
Record yourself for 2 minutes summarizing your week in Croatian; listen back.
Re-drill the pisati family cold.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Listen to your recording — note 2 things to improve.
Recall the week's theme vocabulary.

days.title: Verb family: gledati
days.objective: Master gledati: pogledati, pregledati, razgledati, ugledati.
days.paretoFocus: po- (take a look), pre- (examine), raz- (sightsee), u- (catch sight of) — the prefixes carry the meaning.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the gledati family.
One sentence per member: doctor examines (pregledati), tourist sightsees (razgledati)…
Listen to 1 HRT news item; retell it in 2 sentences.
Quiz yourself: which verb for 'check my email'?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast pogledati vs pregledati aloud.
Review yesterday's conversation notes.

days.title: Tricky present classes I
days.objective: Conjugate -ati > -anem verbs and uzeti/početi in the present.
days.paretoFocus: A small set of irregular present stems (stanem, uzmem, počnem) appears constantly — worth drilling once, properly.
drills: Learn: ostati > ostanem, uzeti > uzmem, početi > počnem.
Conjugate each fully in the present.
Make 6 sentences: 'Počinjem raditi u 8', 'Uzmem kavu i ostanem doma'…
Listen to 1 HRT news item.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Conjugate početi from memory.
Review the gledati family.

days.title: Znanost i izumi — Croatian inventors
days.objective: Read a biography text about a Croatian inventor (exam reading genre).
days.paretoFocus: The exam's Reading A is a ~150-word biography with open questions. Practise the genre on Tesla, Penkala or Vrančić.
drills: Learn 12 science words: znanost, izum, otkriće, istraživanje, pokus…
Read a short biography of Nikola Tesla or Slavoljub Penkala.
Answer 5 who/when/what questions from the text in full sentences.
Listen to 1 HRT news item, ideally science-related.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the inventor's life in 4 sentences.
Recall today's science vocabulary.

days.title: Tricky present classes II
days.objective: Conjugate -sti > -dnem/-tnem/-zem, -ći > -gnem, -ijeti > -esem verbs.
days.paretoFocus: sjednem, padnem, legnem, donesem — high-frequency everyday verbs hiding irregular presents. One focused day fixes them.
drills: Learn: sjesti > sjednem, pasti > padnem, leći > legnem, donijeti > donesem.
Conjugate two of them fully.
Write 6 sentences about your daily routine using them.
Listen to 1 HRT news item.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I'll bring the documents tomorrow' (Sutra ću donijeti dokumente).
Review uzeti and početi presents.

days.title: Listening + speaking: explain an invention
days.objective: Grow to 2 HRT news items/day and explain how something works.
days.paretoFocus: Explaining a process out loud forces connectors (najprije, zatim, na kraju) — exactly what B1 production needs.
drills: Listen to 2 HRT news items today; note main facts of each.
Speaking session: explain to your tutor or spouse how an everyday invention works.
Use najprije / zatim / potom / na kraju at least once each.
Ask your partner one follow-up question in Croatian.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-explain the invention in 3 sentences, faster.
Review present classes from yesterday.

days.title: Writing: an opinion text
days.objective: Write 10 sentences: 'Je li tehnologija poboljšala naš život?'
days.paretoFocus: Opinion texts with s jedne strane / s druge strane structure are the backbone of B1 writing.
drills: Write 10 sentences: intro, 2 arguments for, 1 against, conclusion.
Use mislim da, po mojem mišljenju, s jedne strane / s druge strane.
Include one gledati-family and one pisati-family verb.
Get it checked by your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Read your text aloud; smooth one clumsy sentence.
Recall the opinion connectors.

days.title: Week 23 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate verbs and science theme; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Review + real talk: the two families and the tricky presents must survive contact with conversation.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): science, technology, your favourite invention.
Record 2 minutes: 'Kako je tehnologija promijenila moj život'; listen back.
Cold-test both verb families (pisati, gledati).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 recurring errors from your recording.
Conjugate sjesti and donijeti in the present.

days.title: Verb family: misliti
days.objective: Master misliti: pomisliti, izmisliti, razmisliti, smisliti.
days.paretoFocus: iz- (invent), raz- (think over), s- (come up with) — thinking verbs power every opinion you'll ever give.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the misliti family.
One sentence per member; contrast razmisliti vs smisliti.
Say 'Let me think about it' (Moram razmisliti o tome).
Listen to 1-2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What's the difference between izmisliti and smisliti?
Review opinion connectors from the writing day.

days.title: Glagolski pridjev trpni — the passive participle
days.objective: Form the passive participle: -n / -en / -t (napisan, viđen, otvoren).
days.paretoFocus: The passive participle is everywhere in written Croatian — news, signs, official texts. ASOO lists it as core B1 grammar.
drills: Learn the three endings: napisati > napisan, vidjeti > viđen, otvoriti > otvoren, uzeti > uzet.
Form the participle of 10 verbs you know.
Spot 3 passive participles in any HRT headline today.
Note agreement: napisan/napisana/napisano.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Form the trpni of: pozvati, kupiti, zatvoriti.
Review the misliti family.

days.title: Passive participle in use
days.objective: Use the passive participle in real sentences: 'Roman je napisan 1936.'
days.paretoFocus: Passive sentences let you talk about results without naming the doer — essential for describing art, news and history.
drills: Turn 6 active sentences passive: 'Netko je otvorio muzej' > 'Muzej je otvoren'.
Describe 3 objects around you with participles (kupljen, napravljen, poklonjen…).
Find 3 passives in one HRT article.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'The museum was built in 1880' (Muzej je sagrađen 1880.).
Re-form yesterday's 10 participles cold.

days.title: Umjetnost — art vocabulary + interview reading
days.objective: Talk about art and read an interview (exam reading genre).
days.paretoFocus: Interviews with stances are an exam reading genre; art gives you rich, opinionated material.
drills: Learn 12 art words: slikarstvo, kiparstvo, izložba, umjetnik, djelo, glazba…
Read a short interview with an artist or musician on a Croatian portal.
Note the interviewee's stance on 2 questions.
Meet Croatian artists: Ivan Meštrović, Vlaho Bukovac — one sentence each.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Describe your favourite artwork in 3 sentences.
Review passive participles.

days.title: Retelling a film or book
days.objective: Retell a plot with your reactions — a named B1 exam skill.
days.paretoFocus: 'Retell a book/film and give reactions' is a literal B1 descriptor. Practise it on something you love.
drills: Speaking session: retell a film's plot to your tutor or Croatian spouse (5 min).
Use past tense + 2 passive participles (film je snimljen…, knjiga je napisana…).
Give your reaction: svidjelo mi se jer…, razočarao me kraj…
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the same plot in half the time.
Recall art vocabulary.

days.title: Writing: describe a picture
days.objective: First run of the exam writing task: describe a picture in 7-8 sentences.
days.paretoFocus: Exam task IV.1 is exactly this: people, actions, appearance, setting, what they discuss. Learn the checklist now.
drills: Pick any photo with people; write 7-8 sentences: who, where, what they're doing, how they look, what they might be discussing.
Use present tense + 2 opinion guesses (vjerojatno, čini se da…).
Check every diacritic — the exam is strict.
Get feedback from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite the picture-description checklist from memory.
Fix your 2 biggest errors and re-write those sentences.

days.title: Week 24 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate participles and art; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: The passive participle only sticks if you produce it live — make it appear in today's conversation.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): art, films, books — with retellings.
Record 2 minutes describing a picture aloud; listen back.
Cold-test the misliti family and 10 passive participles.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Form the trpni of 5 random verbs.

days.title: Verb family: raditi
days.objective: Master raditi: uraditi, zaraditi, odraditi, preraditi, obraditi.
days.paretoFocus: za- (earn), od- (get done), pre- (process/rework) — the work-verbs family, perfect fuel for the job-market theme.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the raditi family.
One sentence per member: 'Zaradio sam…', 'Odradila je smjenu…'
Contrast raditi vs uraditi in two sentences.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What does zaraditi vs odraditi mean?
Review passive participles briefly.

days.title: Glagolske imenice — verbal nouns
days.objective: Form and use verbal nouns: čitanje, pisanje, zapošljavanje.
days.paretoFocus: Formal Croatian (news, job ads, contracts) runs on -nje nouns. Recognizing them unlocks written registers.
drills: Form verbal nouns from 10 verbs: učiti > učenje, putovati > putovanje…
Find 5 -nje nouns in one HRT article.
Write 4 sentences: 'Učenje hrvatskog je…', 'Zapošljavanje u Hrvatskoj…'
Note the pattern: verbal nouns are neuter.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Form the verbal noun of: plivati, čitati, stanovati.
Review the raditi family.

days.title: Tržište rada — job ads
days.objective: Read job ads and respond to one (exam skill: responding to ads).
days.paretoFocus: ASOO B1 covers the Croatian job market; the exam expects you to answer ads in writing.
drills: Learn 15 job words: natječaj, poslodavac, plaća, radno iskustvo, uvjeti, prijava…
Read 2 real job ads on a Croatian job portal.
Draft a 5-sentence reply to one ad.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 job-market words.
Say what you do for a living in 3 sentences, formally.

days.title: Writing: your životopis (CV)
days.objective: Write your own CV — exam writing task IV.2, 7-8 sentences.
days.paretoFocus: The exam literally asks for your životopis. Write it once well now; you'll refine it under time pressure later.
drills: Write your biography in 7-8 sentences: birth, education, work, family, languages, hobbies.
Use exact dates and place names in correct cases (u Zagrebu, 1990. godine).
Use 2 raditi-family verbs and 1 verbal noun.
Have your partner check it.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your CV aloud from memory.
Fix the corrections and note recurring errors.

days.title: Speaking: mock job interview
days.objective: Survive a job interview in Croatian.
days.paretoFocus: Interviews force formal register, biography retelling and spontaneous answers — three exam skills in one roleplay.
drills: Roleplay a job interview with your tutor or Croatian spouse (they interview you).
Answer: iskustvo, prednosti/mane, zašto želite ovaj posao?
Ask 2 questions back about the job.
Listen to 2-3 HRT news items today.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-answer 'Zašto želite ovaj posao?' more smoothly.
Review your CV text.

days.title: Instrumental of riječ-type nouns + dob/doba
days.objective: Use riječju/noću forms and distinguish dob (f) from doba (n).
days.paretoFocus: Small ASOO items that show up in exam gap-fills: feminine consonant nouns in instrumental, and the dob/doba trap.
drills: Learn: riječ > riječju, noć > noću, ljubav > ljubavlju, sol > solju.
Write 4 sentences with these instrumentals.
Contrast: 'u mojoj dobi' (age) vs 'godišnje doba' (season).
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What is the instrumental of ljubav?
Review job vocabulary.

days.title: Week 25 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate the work theme; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: You now own the exam's CV genre. Keep it warm while the review locks in verbal nouns.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): work, careers, the Croatian job market.
Record 2 minutes: your work history, from memory; listen back.
Cold-test the raditi family and 5 verbal nouns.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Form riječju-type instrumentals for 3 nouns.

days.title: Verb family: zvati + mock-exam briefing
days.objective: Master zvati (nazvati, pozvati, odazvati se, izazvati) and learn tomorrow's exam format.
days.paretoFocus: Tomorrow you sit your first full mock. Knowing the 5-section format (NN 100/2021) is half the battle.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the zvati family.
One sentence per member: nazvati (phone), pozvati (invite), izazvati (provoke).
Read the mock-exam format in the Quiz tab: 5 sections, ≥60% on listening/reading/grammar, pass/fail writing & speaking.
Early night — treat tomorrow like the real thing.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name the exam's 5 sections from memory.
Contrast pozvati vs nazvati.

days.title: MILESTONE: half-way mock exam
days.objective: Take a full mock exam attempt in the app — all 5 sections.
days.paretoFocus: Half-way check. You won't pass everything yet — the point is a precise map of what stands between you and B1.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: listening, reading, grammar, writing, speaking.
Simulate conditions: no dictionary, no pausing audio (each text plays twice, like the real exam).
Self-grade writing against the model answers honestly.
Note your % per section — the ≥60% rule applies to each of the first three.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write down your score per section.
Pick your weakest section — it gets extra love this month.

days.title: Mock-exam error review
days.objective: Turn every mock-exam mistake into a targeted fix.
days.paretoFocus: An error log is the highest-ROI study artifact that exists. Every miss gets a category and a cure.
drills: Go through every wrong answer; classify: vocabulary / case form / aspect / listening speed / diacritics.
Add every missed word to the Words tab.
Re-do the 5 hardest questions from scratch.
Ask your partner about anything you still don't understand.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your top 3 error categories.
Re-answer 2 of yesterday's missed questions cold.

days.title: Eko-Hrvatska — environment vocabulary
days.objective: Talk about nature, waste and Croatia's national parks.
days.paretoFocus: A 20-hour ASOO theme block. Environment texts are frequent in exam reading and listening material.
drills: Learn 15 eco words: okoliš, otpad, recikliranje, zagađenje, priroda, zaštita…
Name 3 Croatian national parks and say one thing about each (Plitvice, Krka, Mljet).
Listen to 2 HRT news items; one about weather or environment if possible.
Give a 1-minute opinion: 'Recikliram li dovoljno?'

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 eco words.
Review 3 fixes from your error log.

days.title: Besubjektne rečenice & se-constructions
days.objective: Use subjectless sentences: 'Zabranjeno je…', 'Ovdje se ne puši.'
days.paretoFocus: Signs, rules and general truths use se-constructions and subjectless patterns — ASOO B1 grammar you see on every Croatian wall.
drills: Learn the patterns: Zabranjeno je + inf., Treba + inf., Ovdje se + 3rd person.
Write 6 eco-rules: 'Otpad se odvaja', 'Ne smije se bacati smeće u more'…
Translate 4 public signs you remember seeing.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Smoking is forbidden here' two ways.
Review eco vocabulary.

days.title: Listening: half a news bulletin
days.objective: Step up to half an HRT news bulletin in one sitting.
days.paretoFocus: Your listening endurance must grow before exam prep. Half a bulletin today, a full one within a month.
drills: Listen to half an HRT radio news bulletin without pausing.
Write one sentence per story you understood.
Speaking session: discuss an environmental topic with your tutor or spouse.
Re-listen to the hardest story once more.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell 2 news stories from today.
Review se-constructions.

days.title: Week 26 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate the eco theme and mock-exam lessons.
days.paretoFocus: This week produced your error log — the document that steers everything until day 240.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): nature, Croatia's coast and parks, ekologija.
Record 2 minutes: 'Što bih promijenio/promijenila u zaštiti okoliša'; listen back.
Cold-test the zvati family and 5 se-constructions.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-read your error log; check 2 items as fixed.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.

days.title: Verb family: stati
days.objective: Master stati: postati, ostati, prestati, pristati, ustati.
days.paretoFocus: po- (become), o- (stay), pre- (stop), pri- (agree), u- (get up) — five essential meanings from one tiny root.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the stati family.
One sentence per member: 'Prestao sam pušiti', 'Postala je liječnica'…
Note the presents: postanem, ostanem, prestanem.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast prestati vs ostati.
Say 'I get up at 7' (Ustajem u 7).

days.title: Dative experiencer — nositelj stanja u dativu
days.objective: Say how you feel the Croatian way: Hladno mi je, Muka mi je, Sviđa mi se.
days.paretoFocus: Croatian puts the feeler in the dative. This one pattern unlocks dozens of everyday feelings-sentences.
drills: Learn the pattern: dative + je: Hladno mi je, Dosadno joj je, Žao nam je.
Learn sviđati se and nedostajati with the dative.
Write 8 sentences about how family members feel today.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I miss the sea' (Nedostaje mi more).
Review the stati family.

days.title: Ono smo što jedemo — food & health reading
days.objective: Read a health/nutrition article — the exam's gap-fill text genre.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Reading B is a ~150-word health-style article with a 12-word bank. Learn the genre's vocabulary now.
drills: Learn 15 food/health words: prehrana, namirnice, zdravlje, pretilost, vitamini, navike…
Read a short nutrition article on a Croatian portal.
Summarize its advice in 3 sentences.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 food/health words.
Make 3 dative-experiencer sentences about food (Muka mi je od…).

days.title: Word-bank gap-fill training
days.objective: Practise inserting 12 scrambled words into a text in correct case forms.
days.paretoFocus: Gap-fills test vocabulary AND cases at once (travnju, očiju, superlatives). It's the exam's densest section — train it early.
drills: Do a word-bank gap-fill exercise in the Quiz tab (or build one from yesterday's article).
For each gap, name WHY that case form fits.
Watch for traps: locative months (u travnju), genitive plurals (očiju).
Add every miss to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline oko/oči through the plural cases.
Review food vocabulary.

days.title: Speaking: your diet & habits
days.objective: Describe your eating habits and compare them with Croatian ones.
days.paretoFocus: Comparing cultures generates natural B1 speech: opinions, habits, frequencies, likes/dislikes with the dative.
drills: Speaking session: describe your typical eating day to your tutor or Croatian spouse.
Compare with Croatian food culture: marenda/gablec, ručak kao glavni obrok.
Use 4 dative constructions (sviđa mi se, prija mi, ne odgovara mi…).
Listen to half an HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell your partner's eating habits (3rd person practice).
Review gap-fill traps from yesterday.

days.title: Writing: opinion text on food
days.objective: Write 10 sentences: 'Ono smo što jedemo — slažete li se?'
days.paretoFocus: Second opinion text. This time: richer connectors and at least one passive participle and se-construction.
drills: Write 10 sentences with intro, arguments, conclusion.
Include: 1 passive participle, 1 se-construction, 2 dative experiencers.
Vary connectors: osim toga, međutim, stoga, ipak.
Get corrections from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Read your text aloud and fix rhythm.
Note 2 corrections for tomorrow's review.

days.title: Week 27 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate dative experiencers and the food theme.
days.paretoFocus: If 'sviđa mi se' and friends come out automatically today, the week worked.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): food, health, habits — yours and Croatia's.
Record 2 minutes: 'Moja idealna nedjelja s hranom'; listen back.
Cold-test the stati family and 6 dative constructions.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Do 4 quick gap-fill style case questions.

days.title: Verb family: držati
days.objective: Master držati: izdržati, zadržati, održati, podržati.
days.paretoFocus: iz- (endure), za- (keep), o- (hold/maintain), pod- (support) — verbs of persistence, made for sport and debate.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the držati family.
One sentence per member: 'Podržavam tu ideju', 'Održali su sastanak'…
Spot držati-family verbs in one HRT sports article.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast zadržati vs podržati.
Review dative experiencers.

days.title: Pogodbene rečenice — conditional sentences I
days.objective: Build real conditions with ako and unreal ones with kad/da.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO B1 names pogodbene rečenice s veznicima da i kad. Conditions let you speculate — a defining B1 ability.
drills: Real: 'Ako bude lijepo vrijeme, ići ćemo na izlet.'
Unreal: 'Kad bih imao vremena, gledao bih utakmicu.' / 'Da sam znao, došao bih.'
Write 3 sentences of each type about your week.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'If I had money, I would travel' (Da imam novca, putovao/putovala bih).
Review the držati family.

days.title: Conditional sentences II + kondicional refresher
days.objective: Nail bih/bi/bismo/biste forms inside conditional sentences.
days.paretoFocus: The exam's grammar section loves 'bismo' — most natives write it wrong, you won't.
drills: Recite the kondicional of putovati: putovao bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi.
Fix 6 sentences with wrong bi-forms.
Chain conditions: 'Kad bismo živjeli na moru, svaki bismo dan plivali.'
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write the 1st person plural conditional of raditi.
Review yesterday's condition types.

days.title: Sport i mediji — vocabulary + sports news
days.objective: Talk about sport and media; read Croatian sports news.
days.paretoFocus: Sport is Croatia's small talk currency — and sports news is fast, formulaic listening practice.
drills: Learn 15 sport/media words: utakmica, natjecanje, prijenos, vijesti, novinar, gledatelj…
Read one sports article; find 2 passive participles in it.
Name 3 Croatian athletes and their sports (Modrić, Sandra Perković…).
Listen to an HRT sports segment.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 10 sport/media words.
Make 2 conditional sentences about your team winning.

days.title: Speaking: media opinions
days.objective: Debate media habits with polite agreement and disagreement.
days.paretoFocus: Polite (dis)agreement — slažem se, ne bih se složio/složila, imate pravo, ali… — is a named B1 exam skill.
drills: Speaking session: debate 'TV ili internet?' with your tutor or Croatian spouse.
Use 3 agreement/disagreement phrases naturally.
Defend one opinion you don't actually hold (great flexibility training).
Listen to half an HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the polite disagreement phrases.
Review sport vocabulary.

days.title: Writing: a letter retelling an event
days.objective: Write a personal letter retelling a match/concert with feelings.
days.paretoFocus: Exam letters ask for news + retellings + feelings. Combine past tense, aspect and emotion words in one text.
drills: Write a 10-sentence letter about an event you attended (real or imagined).
Include: what happened (aspect pairs), how you felt (dative experiencers), one conditional ('Da si bio tamo…').
Check letter format and diacritics.
Get feedback from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Read the letter aloud; improve one sentence.
Note recurring errors in your error log.

days.title: Week 28 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate conditionals and the sport/media theme.
days.paretoFocus: Conditionals must fire in live speech today — speculation is where B1 conversation gets interesting.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): sport, media, 'što biste radili da…' speculation game.
Record 2 minutes: 'Da mogu promijeniti jednu stvar u svijetu…'; listen back.
Cold-test the držati family and bi-forms.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Produce one ako- and one da-condition cold.

days.title: Verb family: živjeti — day 200!
days.objective: Master živjeti: doživjeti, preživjeti, poživjeti.
days.paretoFocus: 200 days in. doživjeti (experience) and preživjeti (survive) are exactly the verbs to describe this journey.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the živjeti family.
One sentence per member: 'Doživio sam nešto nevjerojatno…'
Write 3 sentences about what you've 'doživio/doživjela' learning Croatian.
Listen to half an HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast doživjeti vs preživjeti.
Celebrate day 200 — say why, in Croatian.

days.title: Zbirne imenice — collective nouns
days.objective: Use djeca, braća, gospoda, lišće with correct agreement.
days.paretoFocus: Collectives look singular but behave specially ('djeca su došla'). ASOO B1 grammar and a classic exam trap.
drills: Learn: dijete > djeca, brat > braća, list > lišće, cvijet > cvijeće.
Practise agreement: 'Djeca su se igrala', 'Braća su visoka'.
Write 5 sentences about family with collectives.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'The children were playing outside'.
Review the živjeti family.

days.title: Verb family: govoriti
days.objective: Master govoriti: razgovarati, dogovoriti se, odgovoriti, izgovoriti, nagovoriti.
days.paretoFocus: The speech-verbs family: converse, agree on, answer, pronounce, persuade — the machinery of every dialogue.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the govoriti family.
One sentence per member: 'Dogovorili smo se za sutra', 'Nagovorila me…'
Notice your textbook's name: Razgovarajte s nama!
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast odgovoriti vs dogovoriti se.
Review collectives (djeca, braća…).

days.title: Obitelj i generacije — reading an interview
days.objective: Read an interview about generational differences and extract stances.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO's family/generations block + the exam's interview reading genre in one session.
drills: Learn 12 words: generacija, razdoblje, odgoj, mladost, starost, razlika…
Read an interview about family or generations on a Croatian portal.
Write down 2 stances the interviewee takes.
Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Summarize the interview in 3 sentences.
Review the govoriti family.

days.title: Listening: a FULL news bulletin
days.objective: Graduate to a full HRT news bulletin + interview your spouse.
days.paretoFocus: Full-bulletin listening is your new daily norm. Real exam audio will feel slow after this.
drills: Listen to a FULL HRT radio news bulletin; note one fact per story.
Interview your Croatian spouse or tutor about their childhood (10 questions).
Ask follow-ups: A zašto? Kako je to izgledalo?
Re-listen to the hardest story.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell your partner's childhood story in 4 sentences.
Recall generation vocabulary.

days.title: Writing: generations text
days.objective: Write about generational differences using collectives and opinions.
days.paretoFocus: Combining this week's grammar (collectives) with opinion structure — compound interest in action.
drills: Write 10 sentences: 'Moja generacija i generacija mojih roditelja'.
Use djeca/braća correctly at least twice.
Include one conditional and one dative experiencer.
Get corrections from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Read your text aloud once.
Update your error log with today's corrections.

days.title: Week 29 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate two verb families and the generations theme.
days.paretoFocus: Nine verb families down, two to go. Today's conversation should surface them all naturally.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): family stories across generations.
Record 2 minutes about your grandparents; listen back.
Cold-test živjeti + govoriti families and 4 collectives.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
List all 9 verb families you've learned so far.

days.title: Verb family: pustiti
days.objective: Master pustiti: dopustiti, otpustiti, propustiti, napustiti, opustiti se.
days.paretoFocus: do- (allow), ot- (fire/release), pro- (miss out), na- (abandon), o-…se (relax) — one root, six life situations.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the pustiti family.
One sentence per member: 'Propustio sam autobus', 'Opusti se!'…
Contrast otpustiti (fire) vs napustiti (leave/abandon).
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'They allowed me to stay' (Dopustili su mi da ostanem).
Review collectives quickly.

days.title: Se-constructions in depth
days.objective: Use the se-passive for general statements: 'Ovdje se govori hrvatski.'
days.paretoFocus: The se-passive is how Croatian says 'one does X' — everywhere in news, rules and recipes. Recognize it, then produce it.
drills: Turn 6 sentences general: 'Ljudi ovdje jedu kasno' > 'Ovdje se jede kasno'.
Write a 5-line recipe using se-forms (dodaje se, miješa se…).
Find 4 se-constructions in one HRT article.
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Croatian is spoken here' (Ovdje se govori hrvatski).
Review the pustiti family.

days.title: Verb family: staviti — the final family
days.objective: Master staviti: dostaviti, predstaviti, nastaviti, ostaviti, sastaviti, postaviti.
days.paretoFocus: The biggest family (deliver, introduce, continue, leave, assemble, set up) — and the last one. All 11 families complete.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the staviti family.
One sentence per member — six sentences.
Contrast ostaviti vs nastaviti vs postaviti.
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Let me introduce my wife/husband' (Predstavljam vam…).
Name all 11 verb families from memory.

days.title: Glagolski prilog sadašnji — verbal adverb
days.objective: Understand and form the present verbal adverb: čitajući, govoreći.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO B1 grammar. You mostly need to READ it (news style: 'Govoreći o krizi, premijer je rekao…') — production is a bonus.
drills: Form: 3rd pl. present + -ći: čitaju > čitajući, govore > govoreći.
Form the adverb of 8 verbs.
Find 2 examples in HRT articles (news style loves them).
Rewrite 3 sentences: 'Dok sam čitao, pio sam kavu' > 'Čitajući, pio sam kavu'.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Form the verbal adverb of pjevati and raditi.
Review the staviti family.

days.title: Ravnopravni ili ne? — equality discussion
days.objective: Discuss equality topics with polite, structured disagreement.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO's ravnopravnost block. Sensitive topics demand precise hedging language — the highest register of B1 speech.
drills: Learn 12 words: prava, jednakost, ravnopravnost, diskriminacija, poštovanje, prilika…
Speaking session: discuss equality at work with your tutor or spouse.
Practise hedges: čini mi se, rekao/rekla bih, možda griješim, ali…
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the hedging phrases.
Review the verbal adverb forms.

days.title: Writing: opinion text on equality
days.objective: Write your strongest opinion text yet — 12 sentences, full structure.
days.paretoFocus: The final Phase 4 writing rep: everything you've learned (participles, se-forms, conditionals, connectors) in one text.
drills: Write 12 sentences: 'Jesu li svi u društvu ravnopravni?'
Checklist: 1 passive participle, 1 se-construction, 1 conditional, 1 collective, 3 connectors.
Proofread diacritics letter by letter.
Get feedback from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Read your text aloud.
Update the error log; check off fixed items.

days.title: Week 30 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate all 11 verb families and this week's grammar.
days.paretoFocus: The verb-family project is complete. Today proves you can deploy them in free conversation.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): society, fairness, work — deliberately using 8+ family verbs.
Record 2 minutes: 'Što se može promijeniti u društvu' (use se-forms); listen back.
Cold-test pustiti + staviti families.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Quiz yourself on one random verb from each of the 11 families.

days.title: Smijeh je lijek — humor + grammar sweep I
days.objective: Enjoy Croatian humor and start the full B1 grammar sweep.
days.paretoFocus: Understanding jokes is real comprehension. Meanwhile, a systematic sweep finds gaps before exam prep begins.
drills: Read/watch 3 simple Croatian jokes or a comedy clip; explain ONE to your spouse in Croatian.
Grammar sweep part 1: aspect pairs, passive participle, verbal nouns — 5 self-test sentences each.
Note anything shaky in your error log.
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell your favourite joke.
Re-drill your shakiest grammar point.

days.title: Phase 4 milestone: B1 core complete
days.objective: Finish the grammar sweep and certify your B1 core. Exam prep starts tomorrow.
days.paretoFocus: 65 days of B1 building are done: 11 verb families, all core grammar, native listening, three writing genres. Now we sharpen for the exam.
drills: Grammar sweep part 2: dative experiencers, conditionals, collectives, se-constructions — 5 self-test sentences each.
Take the B1 grammar quiz in the app.
Record a 3-minute talk: 'Moj put do hrvatskog' — compare with your day 157 recording.
Long conversation with your tutor or spouse to celebrate.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write your 3 weakest areas — exam prep will target them.
Read the exam-prep phase overview in the app.

days.title: Exam prep begins: format mastery + listening A
days.objective: Know the exam inside-out and drill short-sentence listening.
days.paretoFocus: 25 days to exam-ready. The format (NN 100/2021): 5 sections, ≥60% each on listening/reading/grammar, pass/fail writing & speaking. No surprises allowed.
drills: Study the mock exam in the Quiz tab: section order, task types, points.
Listening drill I.A: six short sentences (heard twice), open short answers — traffic-report style.
Listen to a full HRT bulletin; answer 'koliko/gdje/kada' about 3 stories.
Start a fresh exam-phase error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name all 5 exam sections and the pass rule.
Re-answer your 2 missed listening items.

days.title: Listening B: DA/NE statements
days.objective: Master the longer-text listening task with true/false statements.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Listening B: one longer text (event announcement), heard twice, six DA/NE statements. Strategy: read statements BEFORE the audio starts.
drills: Do the DA/NE listening drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Strategy: first listen = gist, second listen = verify each statement.
Listen to an HRT event/culture announcement; invent 4 DA/NE statements and answer them.
Log misses in the error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the two-listen strategy.
Retell today's announcement in 3 sentences.

days.title: Reading A: biography + open questions
days.objective: Answer open questions on a ~150-word biography quickly and precisely.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Reading A: biography text, seven open questions. Answers must come FROM the text — no creativity, full sentences, correct forms.
drills: Do the biography reading drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab (Marija Jurić Zagorka style).
Practise locating answers: scan for names, dates, places first.
Write answers as full sentences with correct case endings.
Read one more short biography online for speed.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the biography in 4 sentences.
Review 2 errors from the log.

days.title: Reading B: word-bank gap fill
days.objective: Insert 12 bank words into a text in their exact case forms.
days.paretoFocus: The densest exam task: 12 points, vocabulary + case forms together (travnju, očiju, superlatives). Diacritic-perfect answers only.
drills: Do the word-bank gap-fill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
For each gap: identify the needed case BEFORE choosing the word.
Re-do every miss and say WHY the correct form fits.
Add missed forms to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline mjesec and oko in the tricky forms.
Recall 3 gap-fill traps.

days.title: Grammar A: form choice
days.objective: Choose correct forms: genitive plural, futur I spelling, bismo, prepositions.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Grammar A samples: '4 i pol milijuna stanovnika', 'ići ćemo', 'bismo', 'među' + instrumental, 'o njemu', 'ljepše'. Drill these exact patterns.
drills: Do the grammar form-choice drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Review: genitive plural after numbers/quantities.
Review: futur I orthography (ići ćemo vs radit ćemo) and bih/bismo.
Log every miss with its rule.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write 'we would go' and 'we will go' correctly.
Say '5 million inhabitants' with correct genitive.

days.title: Grammar B: aspect & prefix verbs
days.objective: Choose the right prefixed/aspect verb in context — your verb families pay off.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Grammar B is exactly the ASOO 'glagoli u kontekstu' material: napisala vs zapisala vs opisala. You trained 11 families for this.
drills: Do the aspect/prefix-verb drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Words tab: verb-families pack — full cold review of all 11 families.
For 8 sentences, choose the family verb and explain the prefix's meaning.
Log any family that still wobbles.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
One sentence each: opisati, propustiti, podržati.
Recall the meaning of prefixes iz-, pre-, do-.

days.title: Writing under time: describe a picture
days.objective: Write the 7-8 sentence picture description in 20 minutes flat.
days.paretoFocus: Exam writing task 1: opis slike — people, actions, appearance, setting, what they discuss. Time pressure is the skill now.
drills: Set a 20-minute timer; do the picture-description task from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Self-check against the rubric: all checklist points covered? 7-8 sentences? Diacritics?
Rewrite your 2 weakest sentences.
Get one native check from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite the picture checklist from memory.
Note timing: did you finish with minutes to spare?

days.title: Writing under time: životopis
days.objective: Write your 7-8 sentence CV in 20 minutes, error-free.
days.paretoFocus: Exam writing task 2 is your own biography. You wrote it on day 175 — now it must flow out under pressure, perfectly spelled.
drills: 20-minute timer: write the životopis task from the mock exam in the Quiz tab, WITHOUT looking at your day-175 version.
Compare with your polished version; merge the best of both.
Have your Croatian spouse do a final read.
Memorize your 3 hardest spellings (dates, place names).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your CV aloud from memory.
Check every diacritic in today's text once more.

days.title: Speaking simulation #1
days.objective: Run a full oral-exam simulation.
days.paretoFocus: The oral exam is live conversation: introduce yourself, answer questions, discuss everyday topics. Simulate the pressure now, not on exam day.
drills: Full speaking simulation with your tutor (or spouse playing strict examiner): intro, biography questions, opinion questions, a picture to describe aloud.
Use the speaking prompts from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Record it; self-grade with the rubric: fluency, grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation.
Note 3 hesitation points to smooth out.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-answer your worst question, better.
Prepare mentally: tomorrow is full mock exam #1.

days.title: FULL TIMED MOCK EXAM #1
days.objective: Sit the complete exam under real conditions.
days.paretoFocus: Everything at once, timed, no aids. Target: ≥60% on listening, reading and grammar + pass both writing and speaking.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: all 5 sections, one sitting, timed.
Real conditions: audio plays twice only, no dictionary, no pausing.
Write both writing tasks by hand if possible (the real exam is on paper).
Record your speaking answers; grade everything honestly against the rubrics.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write down your % per section next to the day-180 scores.
Rest — analysis is tomorrow, not tonight.

days.title: Mock #1 error-log review
days.objective: Dissect mock #1 and pick your two target sections.
days.paretoFocus: The gap between mock #1 and mock #2 is where the exam is won. Every error gets a category, a rule and a re-test date.
drills: Classify every miss: vocabulary / case / aspect / listening / diacritics / time pressure.
Compare with day 180: celebrate the growth, circle what's stuck.
Choose the 2 weakest sections — days 227-234 target them.
Add all missed words/forms to your notes; ask your partner about anything unclear.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your 2 target sections and why.
Re-do the 3 hardest questions from mock #1.

days.title: Weak-section day: listening intensive
days.objective: Double dose of exam-style listening.
days.paretoFocus: Twice-heard audio rewards note-taking on the first pass and verification on the second. Build that reflex.
drills: Re-do both listening drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab (short sentences + DA/NE).
Full HRT bulletin: write one fact per story, numbers included.
Dictation: transcribe 3 sentences from HRT audio word-for-word.
Log listening errors: was it speed, vocabulary or numbers?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 3 large numbers you heard today.
Retell the bulletin's top story.

days.title: Weak-section day: reading intensive
days.objective: Both exam reading genres, back-to-back, under time.
days.paretoFocus: Reading is 19 of the 42 written points — the biggest scored section. Speed + case-form precision decide it.
drills: Timed: biography + open questions from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Timed: word-bank gap-fill from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Read one HRT article; summarize in 3 sentences.
Log every miss with the rule that fixes it.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 3 gap-fill case traps.
Review yesterday's listening notes.

days.title: Weak-section day: grammar re-drill
days.objective: Re-drill every grammar structure you've missed since day 216.
days.paretoFocus: Your error log knows exactly which structures leak points. Fix the leaks, not the whole ocean.
drills: Re-do every grammar item marked wrong in your error log.
Words tab: verb-families pack — target the 3 wobbliest families.
Write 6 sentences using your personal top-3 error structures correctly.
Do one fresh grammar drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State your top-3 grammar rules in your own words.
Check off fixed errors in the log.

days.title: Writing polish: both genres back-to-back
days.objective: Both writing tasks in one 40-minute sitting — like the real exam.
days.paretoFocus: On exam day you write opis + životopis in one session. Train the stamina and the switch between genres.
drills: 40-minute timer: picture description THEN životopis, from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Self-check both against rubrics; count sentences (7-8 each).
Fix diacritics with a coloured pen — make errors visible.
One native check on the weaker text.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Which genre cost more time? Plan the split.
Rewrite your single weakest sentence.

days.title: Speaking simulation #2
days.objective: Second full oral simulation — smoother, calmer, longer answers.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners reward answers of 3+ sentences. Train expanding: statement + reason + example, every time.
drills: Full speaking simulation with your tutor or Croatian spouse using the mock exam speaking prompts in the Quiz tab.
Rule: every answer gets 3+ sentences (statement, reason, example).
Record and compare with day 224 — hear the difference.
Drill your 3 hesitation points from last time.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Expand 'Volim li Hrvatsku?' into 4 sentences, aloud.
Note 2 remaining rough spots.

days.title: Listening endurance day
days.objective: A full bulletin + a longer audio segment + exam drills in one day.
days.paretoFocus: Exam audio will feel easy if HRT feels normal. Overtrain the ear this week.
drills: Full HRT bulletin in the morning: one fact per story.
One longer HRT audio segment (10+ min) on a familiar theme in the afternoon.
Re-do the DA/NE listening drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab — aim for 6/6.
Shadow-speak 5 sentences right after the anchor says them.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the audio segment in 4 sentences.
Review any number you misheard today.

days.title: Reading speed day
days.objective: Two full exam reading tasks under a strict clock.
days.paretoFocus: Slow reading steals time from writing. Target: finish each reading task with 25% of its time to spare for checking.
drills: Timed re-run: both reading tasks from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Then: skim one HRT article in 2 minutes and answer who/what/when.
Practise the scan: names, dates and numbers jump out first.
Log timing per task.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Did you beat your day-228 times?
Review 3 vocabulary items that slowed you down.

days.title: Grammar final sweep
days.objective: A last full pass over the exam's grammar catalogue — strict diacritics.
days.paretoFocus: Tomorrow is mock #2. Tonight your grammar must be boring: genitive plurals, bismo, ćemo-spelling, prefixed verbs — automatic.
drills: Do both grammar drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab with strict-diacritics mode.
Speed round: 10 aspect choices from the verb families, 30 seconds each.
Re-check your error log: every item should be checked off or re-drilled today.
Early night — mock #2 tomorrow.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write 'ići ćemo', 'radit ćemo', 'bismo išli' correctly.
Name the 5 exam sections and the pass rule one more time.

days.title: FULL TIMED MOCK EXAM #2
days.objective: The dress rehearsal: pass all 5 sections.
days.paretoFocus: This is the go/no-go signal. ≥60% on listening, reading and grammar + two passes = you're ready to book the real thing.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: all 5 sections, timed, one sitting, real conditions.
Writing by hand; speaking recorded and rubric-graded.
No aids, audio twice only — exactly like exam day.
Note scores next to mock #1 and day 180.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Log your % per section.
Rest. Analysis tomorrow.

days.title: Mock #2 error-log review + readiness call
days.objective: Compare mock #2 vs mock #1 and confirm your readiness picture.
days.paretoFocus: Three data points (day 180, 225, 235) show your trajectory. Anything still under 60% gets the final four days.
drills: Full error review: classify, compare with mock #1, celebrate every fixed category.
Check the pass rule per section: ≥60% × 3 + writing pass + speaking pass?
Pick the ONE weakest remaining section for day 237.
Add final missed items to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State your section scores from memory.
Write tomorrow's plan in one sentence.

days.title: Final targeting: your weakest section
days.objective: One focused day on whatever section scored lowest in mock #2.
days.paretoFocus: Generic review is over. Today is surgical: one section, its task types, its traps, until it clears 60% comfortably.
drills: Re-do that section's drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab twice.
Re-read the error-log entries for that section; drill each rule.
If it's listening: extra HRT bulletin. If reading/grammar: extra timed text. If writing/speaking: one more timed rep.
Finish with one confidence rep of your STRONGEST section.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Did the weak section clear 60% today?
Review 3 items from the error log.

days.title: Writing final: clean copies
days.objective: One perfect, timed copy of each writing genre.
days.paretoFocus: Writing is pass/fail — one clean, complete, 7-8 sentence text per genre is all that's asked. Make 'clean' your default.
drills: Timed: one picture description + one životopis (mock exam tasks in the Quiz tab), by hand.
Proofread pass 1: verb endings and cases. Pass 2: every č/ć/š/ž/đ.
Final native check by your Croatian spouse.
File both as your reference 'exam-day templates'.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your životopis one last time.
Recall the picture-description checklist.

days.title: Speaking final + exam-day strategy
days.objective: Last speaking simulation plus a calm exam-day playbook.
days.paretoFocus: You know the content. Today locks in composure: strategies for twice-heard audio, answer order, and what to do when a word won't come.
drills: Final relaxed speaking simulation with your tutor or Croatian spouse — enjoy it.
Rehearse recovery phrases: 'Kako da to kažem…', 'Mislim, htio/htjela sam reći…'
Write your exam-day playbook: listening (notes on pass 1, verify on pass 2), reading (scan first), writing (draft, count sentences, proofread diacritics).
Light HRT listening only — no cramming.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your exam-day playbook.
Sleep well — tomorrow you certify readiness.

days.title: FINAL: readiness check + book the real exam
days.objective: Confirm exam readiness in the app and book the official B1 exam.
days.paretoFocus: 240 days from the alphabet to a B1 booking. The certificate (NN 100/2021) is the legal proof of language for long-term residence and the citizenship pathway.
drills: Open the exam-readiness view in Progress: confirm all 5 sections passed and tick the can-do checklist.
Book the real exam: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS or the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies — contact them about 10 days ahead and register with your ID.
Check practicalities: date, fee, documents, location.
Celebrate in Croatian with your spouse — you earned it: Uspio/Uspjela sam!

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say, in Croatian, what you'll do after passing the exam.
Keep the daily HRT habit until exam day — the ear must stay warm.
